homepage.h1: Perran Moon is the MP for Camborne, Redruth and Hayle - working for a better life for everyone.
homepage.meta_description: Welcome to the official website of Perran Moon MP.
headings: h3: Jobs in the green economy
h3: Fox Hunting and Animal Cruelty
homepage.text_excerpt: Home - Perran Moon MP Skip to main content Hit enter to search or ESC to close Close Search Menu Menu Perran Moon is the MP for Camborne, Redruth and Hayle - working for a better life for everyone. Meet Perran My name is Perran Moon. I have been the Member of Parliament for Camborne, Redruth and Hayle since July 2024. I was born and raised in Redruth. My Dad was a local GP and my Mum was a nurse at Treliske Hospital. I am particularly focused on ensuring that our local constituency receives the funding and support that it deserves, whilst also promoting economic growth through sectors like critical minerals and renewable energy. I have been particularly pleased with the Government's investment in South Crofty Tin Mine at the centre of my constituency. CONTACT ME How to Contact Perran Perran is here to help you, if you would like to get in touch then please click the button below and a member of my team will be in touch. For a list of issues that Perran can and cannot get involved with, please see the FAQs page. Contact Me FAQs FAQs FAQs FAQs FAQs Click below if you have a question. We may already have an answer for you. FAQs POLICIES Cost of Living Crisis Unfortunately, our constituency suffers from large levels of poverty and deprivation. As a MP, I am delighted that this Labour Government is taking action including increasing the minimum wage, offering free school breakfast clubs and increasing access to free school meals. Housing The Cornish Housing Crisis has been fuelled by second homes and Airbnbs. I have been supporting the ‘First Homes not Second Homes’ initiative since it began in order to fix the system that favours the rich over the poor – so that everyone can afford to live in Cornwall. Health & Social Care As the son of a doctor and a nurse, it pains me to see the state of the NHS. I am working tirelessly to push towards a new health and social care system that ensures the right care is delivered, at the right time to the right people. Jobs in the green economy Economic rejuvenation should be based on maximising sustainable energy resources to create secure, well-paid jobs. The opportunities in Cornwall are immense, and I am determined that Camborne, Redruth and Hayle will be a key part of unleashing the Cornish Celtic Tiger Devolution We need Cornish solutions to Cornish challenges. To achieve this we need a devolution deal that empowers us to manage our own destiny, including the ability to deal with the Cornish housing crisis, investment in culture, language and heritage, and fund our economic growth. Education We owe it to our young people to ensure that all our educational institutions offer excellence for all . I will continue to work with this Labour Government to undo the mess left to us by the previous Conservative government to ensure that everyone can get the opportunities they deserve. Brexit & Europe For economic, social, cultural and security reasons, I will push for the closest possible relationship with our nearest neighbours, friends and allies. I believe that our economic destiny is intrinsically linked with that of the European Union. Mental Health Care This is an issue very close to my heart because, as a parent, I have been through the trauma of the Child and Adult Mental Health Service (CAMHS). The system was crippled by a decade of underfunding. Fixing it is one of my primary concerns as MP. Environment Our job is to care for, protect and nurture our natural environment. As an MP, I will work tirelessly to make sure that our environment is protected, particularly by ending the pumping of raw sewage into our waterways. Electoral Reform It’s up to politicians to galvanise the electorate to come out and vote. But we currently have an electoral system that disenfranchises a large part of the electorate, this is why I support reform to our current electoral system. Fox Hunting and Animal Cruelty Quite simply, zero tolerance to animal cruelty in a modern progressive Britain. Having lived in rural

pages.title: Contact - Perran Moon MP
pages.meta_description: If you are resident in the Camborne, Redruth and Hayle constituency, please use these links to contact Perran Moon MP.
headings: h3: Please use one of the methods listed below
h2: Perran is here to help everyone
h3: What if Perran isn't your MP?
pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Perran Moon MP Skip to main content Hit enter to search or ESC to close Close Search Menu Menu Contact me Please use one of the methods listed below Perran is here to help everyone Perran welcomes contact from all his constituents. There are a number of ways to get in touch. Email Perran Perran's Instagram Perran on Facebook Call Perran's Constituency Office: 01209206046 Notes for contacting Perran Timeframes for a reply Due to the large number of emails, letters and phone calls each day, I will try and respond to you as quickly as possible. Please note that all communications, including emails, are dealt with in the order in which they are received. We currently have a significant volume of casework coming in, and whilst we generally aim to get back within two weeks, at present it will take significantly longer. Have you included your full home address? If not please re-send your email including this information in order to receive a response. This is requested even if you email frequently. What if Perran isn't your MP? Parliamentary Protocol prevents an MP from dealing with enquiries form another MP’s constituency, so be sure that you are a constituent of Camborne and Redruth, as contacting the wrong representative can cause delays to your case. You can check online here: FindYourMP If your query relates to a Local Authority issue then please direct your correspondence directly to your locally elected Cornwall Councillor. To find your local representative please visit: MyLocalCouncillor © 2026 Perran Moon MP Terms & Conditions Privacy Policy facebook instagram phone email Close Menu Home About Me News Policies Constituency Activity Parliamentary Activity FAQs Gallery Contact facebook instagram phone email

pages.title: About Me - Perran Moon MP
pages.meta_description: A bit about Perran Moon, Labour MP for Camborne, Redruth and Hayle. (Page still under maintenance so please see @Perran4CRH on FB).
pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Perran Moon MP Skip to main content Hit enter to search or ESC to close Close Search Menu Menu About Me A bit about Perran... My name is Perran Moon, I have been the Member of Parliament for Camborne, Redruth and Hayle since July 2024. I was born and raised in Redruth. My dad was a GP in the local area. Before Parliament, I worked primarily in the Electric Vehicle sector for companies like Renault, Nissan, Autotrader, Manheim, ZapMap and Believ. I also have had experience working in local government prior to my nomination as a candidate in the 2024 general election. © 2026 Perran Moon MP Terms & Conditions Privacy Policy facebook instagram phone email Close Menu Home About Me News Policies Constituency Activity Parliamentary Activity FAQs Gallery Contact facebook instagram phone email

pages.title: Parliamentary Activity - Perran Moon MP
pages.meta_description: A record of Perran Moon's Parliamentary Activity, working for the people of Camborne, Redruth and Hayle in Westminster.
headings: h2: Perran's spoken and written contributions working for you in Westminster
pages.text_excerpt: Parliamentary Activity - Perran Moon MP Skip to main content Hit enter to search or ESC to close Close Search Menu Menu Spoken Contributions 155 Written Questions 198 PARLIAMENTARY ACTIVITIES Perran's spoken and written contributions working for you in Westminster Recent Contributions Here are my recent Parliamentary contributions. See Older Contributions Written Question: Department for Business and Trade - Pay (20/10/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to help support people who were underpaid wages by their former employers. See More Education: Children with SEND - Mainstream Schools (20/10/2025) A statement regarding the adversarial nature of the SEND system for all stakeholders and a move to focus on inclusion as a key measure of success. CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE See More ENGLISH DEVOLUTION AND COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT BILL COMMITTEE (Day 2 - 16/10/2025) Please click here for a collection of statements and questions made by Perran Moon on Day 2 of the English Devolution Bill Committee. See More Written Question: Department for Business and Trade - Funerals: Small Businesses (15/10/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what support her Department is providing small independent funeral directors in response to market pressures from large direct cremation providers? See More ENGLISH DEVOLUTION AND COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT BILL COMMITTEE (Day 1 - 14/10/2025) Please click here for a collection of statements and questions made by Perran Moon on Day 1 of the English Devolution Bill Committee. See More Bovine Tuberculosis Control and Badger Culling (13/10/2025) A speech in a Westminster Hall debate following on from a petition called "End the Badger cull and adopt other approaches to bovine TB control". The petition received 389 signatures from Camborne, Redruth and Hayle. See More Written Question: Treasury - Financial Ombudsman Service: Standards (13/10/2025) To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she is taking to tackle the backlog of cases at the Financial Ombudsman Service. See More Written Question: Treasury - Life Insurance (13/10/2025) To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what regulatory safeguards are in place to protect consumers from excessive premium increases following the transfer of life insurance policy liabilities. See More Written Question: Treasury - Life Insurance (13/10/2025) To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps her Department is taking to regulate the pricing of legacy life insurance policies following provider transfers. See More Housing, Communities and Local Government Questions (13/10/2025) A question asking for reassurances that unleashing the Cornish Celtic Tiger and maximising our economic potential is not reliant on Cornwall joining a Mayoral Combined Authority. CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE. See More Written Question: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs - Cats and Dogs: Smuggling (08/10/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what will be in the terms of the Carr Hill review. See More Written Question: Department for Education - Children: Community Development (23/09/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to improve community (a) understanding and (b) acceptance of (i) residential and (ii) support services for vulnerable children (A) in the community and (B) in the criminal justice system. See More Written Question: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology - Animal Experiments: Regulation (19/09/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of banning animal testing.
